tluaMetatableStatement.tlua(22,18): error TLUA2339: Property 'describe' does not exist on type '{ count: (self: table) => number; n: number; }'.
tluaMetatableStatement.tlua(29,18): error TLUA2339: Property 'describe' does not exist on type '{ n: number; }'.
tluaMetatableStatement.tlua(36,18): error TLUA2339: Property 'describe' does not exist on type '{ n: number; }'.
tluaMetatableStatement.tlua(45,18): error TLUA2339: Property 'describe' does not exist on type '{ n: number; }'.
tluaMetatableStatement.tlua(54,18): error TLUA2339: Property 'describe' does not exist on type '{ n: number; }'.
tluaMetatableStatement.tlua(67,18): error TLUA2339: Property 'describe' does not exist on type '{ n: number; }'.
tluaMetatableStatement.tlua(83,18): error TLUA2339: Property 'describe' does not exist on type '{ n: number; }'.


==== tluaMetatableStatement.tlua (7 errors) ====
    // `setmetatable(t, mt);` as a statement -- how Lua actually attaches a metatable, mutating in
    // place -- narrows its table operand to the paired type, the way an assertion narrows its
    // argument.
    
    local Helpers = { describe = function(self: table): string return "d"; end };
    local Counters = { count = function(self: table): number return 1; end };
    local mt = { __index = Helpers };
    local mt2 = { __index = Counters };
    
    function basic(): void
      local t = { n = 1 };
      setmetatable(t, mt);
      local d: string = t.describe(t);  // the fallback is visible after the statement
      local n: number = t.n;            // the own member is intact
    end
    
    function replaced(): void
      local t = { n = 1 };
      setmetatable(t, mt);
      setmetatable(t, mt2);             // replacement: unpair, then re-pair
      local c: number = t.count(t);
      local gone = t.describe;          // the old pairing is gone
                     ~~~~~~~~
!!! error TLUA2339: Property 'describe' does not exist on type '{ count: (self: table) => number; n: number; }'.
    end
    
    function detached(): void
      local t = { n = 1 };
      setmetatable(t, mt);
      setmetatable(t, nil);             // nil detaches
      local gone = t.describe;
                     ~~~~~~~~
!!! error TLUA2339: Property 'describe' does not exist on type '{ n: number; }'.
    end
    
    function invalidated(): void
      local t = { n = 1 };
      setmetatable(t, mt);
      t = { n = 2 };                    // an assignment kills the narrowing
      local gone = t.describe;
                     ~~~~~~~~
!!! error TLUA2339: Property 'describe' does not exist on type '{ n: number; }'.
    end
    
    function inLoop(cond: boolean): void
      local t = { n = 1 };
      while cond do
        setmetatable(t, mt);
        local d: string = t.describe(t);
      end
      local gone = t.describe;          // the never-ran arm keeps t unpaired
                     ~~~~~~~~
!!! error TLUA2339: Property 'describe' does not exist on type '{ n: number; }'.
    end
    
    function inConditional(cond: boolean): void
      local t = { n = 1 };
      if cond then
        setmetatable(t, mt);
        local d: string = t.describe(t);
      end
      local gone = t.describe;          // the join keeps the unpaired arm
                     ~~~~~~~~
!!! error TLUA2339: Property 'describe' does not exist on type '{ n: number; }'.
    end
    
    function propertyTarget(): void
      local holder = { field = { n = 1 } };
      setmetatable(holder.field, mt);   // a property-access target narrows too
      local d: string = holder.field.describe(holder.field);
    end
    
    function shadowed(): void
      local setmetatable = function(value: table, metatable: table): table return value; end;
      local t = { n = 1 };
      setmetatable(t, mt);              // not the global, so no narrowing
      local gone = t.describe;
                     ~~~~~~~~
!!! error TLUA2339: Property 'describe' does not exist on type '{ n: number; }'.
    end
    
    function constCapture(): void
      local t = { n = 1 };
      setmetatable(t, mt);
      local f = function(): void
        // t is never reassigned, so the narrowing reaches into the closure, as an assertion's does.
        local d: string = t.describe(t);
      end;
    end
    
    function assignForm(): void
      local t = { n = 1 };
      local r = setmetatable(t, mt);    // the assign form types the result, not the argument
      local rd: string = r.describe(r);
      local gone = t.describe;
                     ~~~~~~~~
!!! error TLUA2339: Property 'describe' does not exist on type '{ n: number; }'.
    end
    
    // The metatable argument references the table through the same statement, inside a loop: the
    // re-entrancy must terminate (the inner query simply sees the unpaired table).
    function selfReferential(cond: boolean): void
      local t = { n = 1, mt = mt };
      while cond do
        setmetatable(t, t.mt);
      end
      local after: number = t.n;
    end
